title: Glasgow Prestwick Airport is being used by Trump to bomb Iran, says military intelligence expert

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Add Yahoo as a preferred source to see more of our stories on Google. -Credit:Alasdair MacLeod/Daily Record (Alasdair MacLeod/Daily Record) Glasgow Prestwick Airport is being used by Donald Trump's forces to facilitate its bombing of Iran, according to a leading expert in military intelligence. Philip Ingram, a former Army Colonel and Intelligence Officer, has said the Scottish Government owned facility will “almost certainly” be supporting the war. It comes after John Swinney said he would consider banning US air force flights landing at the airport. America and Israel have threatened to intensify strikes in the Middle East which are estimated to have killed over 1,500 people so far and could plunge the world economy into recession, the Sunday Mail reports.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Speaking to the Sunday Mail, First Minister John Swinney has demanded an end to the war and warned it could send energy and food prices soaring. Five historical Glasgow heroines to remember this International Women’s Day The 19 safest places in UK if WW3 erupts as experts warns 'avoid Scottish spot' He said: “These are deeply concerning times, and I know people in Scotland will be worried sick for friends and family in the Gulf, and worried sick about the threat of rising food and energy costs if this war continues.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Glasgow Prestwick Airport is being used by Trump to bomb Iran, says military intelligence expert
